verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Cantinho da Baia After work (16:30 start)
verdict.reason: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide is a bit awkward for the bay, so stay cautious.

conversationalSummary.text: Cantinho da Baia at After work (16:30 start) looks like the best option, but it is still a check-first call. There is enough potential for a useful session, but the lack of push could spoil it. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam before leaving.

photo.alt: Photo of Cantinho da Baia surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Cantinho da Baia looks like a fun-sized window for Safe fun, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 158 kJ, 9 s period, consistent 75/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A forgiving sandy bay with lots of take-off points; this is best for easy wave count and cleaner faces when the north wind is bothering exposed beaches; 6-9s at high tide is too weak and wrap-starved in the sheltered bay; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: 6-9s at high tide is too weak and wrap-starved in the sheltered bay; gusts over 45 km/h add chop risk.
best.shortExplanation: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide is a bit awkward for the bay, so stay cautious.
warnings: 6-9s at high tide is too weak and wrap-starved in the sheltered bay
gusts over 45 km/h add chop risk

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Meio da Baia looks like a solid window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 1.2-1.5 m, 409 kJ, 9 s period, consistent 71/100, and wind is the main risk.  An inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay; this is best for bodyboard sessions when the bay is clean, there is enough period and size, and the forecast shows a defined wave; 6-9s at low tide can still make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia punchy.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Meio da Baia is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a solid window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 1.2-1.5 m, 287 kJ, 9 s period, consistent 70/100, and wind is the main risk.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at low tide can still make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia punchy.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.

ranked.explanation: Baleal looks like a fun-sized window for Safe fun, Low confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 128 kJ, 9 s period, consistent 74/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the Baleal sandbanks; gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy.
ranked.shortExplanation: Baleal is worth a quick check for after work (18:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is part of the picture, but not the deciding factor.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the Baleal sandbanks
gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy
